mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate indicates strengthening labor demand and rising personal incomes, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If walk-forward Rank IC of UNRATE (inverted) against 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 in any 2-year window, or if instances of declining UNRATE are followed by negative returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, tight monetary policy restricts credit availability and raises the cost of capital, which depresses investment and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If in more than 40% of triggered dates (FF>5%) the subsequent 60-day return is positive, the hypothesis is refuted. Also, if the rank correlation between FF level and forward returns is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between declining housing starts and still-rising industrial production signals an unbalanced economy where housing weakness will eventually drag down overall activity, predicting negative equity returns over the short term as markets reprice risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST year-over-year decline > 5% and INDPRO year-over-year growth > 2%
failure_condition: If the joint signal produces positive returns in more than half of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, or if the spread between HOUST and INDPRO growth rates has no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds the Federal Reserve's implicit target of 2%, further increases in CPI indicate overheating and force the Fed to tighten monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: If in months where CPI yoy exceeds 2%, the median 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) compared to months with lower inflation, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures (as proxied by retail sales) reflect strong consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If the correlation between monthly RSAFS growth and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (rank IC < 0.03) over full sample.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 110 index points signals a contraction in manufacturing activity, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and depresses equity prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If triggered instances show a median 60-day return of zero or positive, the hypothesis fails. Rejection if hit rate of negative returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of very low unemployment and strong GDP growth signals that the economy is operating above potential, leading to capacity constraints and eventual policy tightening, predicting negative equity returns over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0 and GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth > 3%
failure_condition: If at triggered dates the subsequent 20-day return is positive more than 60% of the time, or if the interaction term in a regression is not statistically significant over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions when the unemployment rate is already low, a deceleration in payroll employment growth signals that labor demand is faltering, which precedes a downturn in consumption and earnings, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: If triggered events are followed by positive 5-day returns more than half the time, or if the signal has no consistent predictive power in out-of-sample testing.
